title: System Hacking
description: To discover hosts, services, and vulnerabilities efficiently in CTF environments, System Hacking is the foundation of any penetration test.
concept: System hacking is a multi-stage process starting with discovery and enumeration. Nmap is the industry standard for identifying open ports and service versions. For stealthier local network discovery, Netdiscover passively listens for ARP requests to find live hosts. Successful system hacking relies on meticulous scanning to identify weak services like outdated Apache servers, misconfigured SMB shares, or exposed RPC ports.

title: Web Hacking
description: Transforming web enumeration into exploitation by identifying hidden paths and vulnerable parameters.
concept: Web hacking follows a rigorous five-stage cycle: Reconnaissance (passive/active), Scanning & Enumeration (identifying versions), Gaining Access (exploiting vulnerabilities like SQLi/XSS), Maintaining Access (web shells), and Covering Tracks. Essential techniques include directory bursting with Gobuster to find hidden admin panels or backup files, and vulnerability scanning with Nikto to detect outdated software or insecure headers.

tools.name: Enumeration & Recon
tools.description: Intelligence gathering for web applications.

tools.description: Discovering hidden endpoints and parameters.

title: File Hacking
description: Extracting hidden data from PDFs, VHDs, and binary files.
concept: File hacking often involves looking for 'magic bytes' to identify real file types, extracting strings, or mounting virtual drives to find sensitive data.

tools.name: Data Extraction
tools.description: Tools to uncover hidden information in files.

tools.name: Disk Forensics
tools.description: Analyzing virtual disks and filesystems.

commands.usage: Lists files along with their hidden data streams.

title: Cryptography
description: Decrypting classic ciphers and modern encryption schemes.
concept: Common ciphers include Caesar (shift), Vigenere (polyalphabetic), and XOR-based One-Time Pads. Frequency analysis is your best friend.

tools.name: Encoding & Ciphers
tools.description: Tools for data transformation.

title: Forensics
description: Investigating artifacts in images, memory, and network captures.
concept: Forensics involves identifying hidden data in images (Steganography), recovering deleted files from disk images, and analyzing USB keystroke traffic in PCAP files.

tools.name: Image Forensics
tools.description: Analyzing image files for hidden data.

tools.name: Network & USB
tools.description: Traffic and peripheral analysis.

title: Password Cracking
description: Breaking hashes and protected archives using brute-force and dictionary attacks.
concept: Password cracking is the process of recovering passwords from data that has been stored in or transmitted by a computer system in scrambled form (hashes).

tools.name: Hash Cracking
tools.description: Recovering passwords from cryptographic hashes.

commands.description: Crack hashes with John the Ripper

tools.name: File & Key Cracking
tools.description: Decrypting protected files and keys.

title: Privilege Escalation
description: Escalating from a low-privileged shell to root or SYSTEM access.
concept: Privilege escalation focuses on misconfigurations, weak permissions, sudo rights, and kernel exploits. Automate the process using scripts like LinPEAS or WinPEAS.

tools.name: Enumeration
tools.description: Finding escalation vectors.

tools.name: Techniques
tools.description: Common methods for escalation.

title: Metasploit Framework
description: What Is Metasploit? Metasploit is a popular open-source framework for creating, testing, and deploying exploits.
concept: Metasploit's collection of exploits, payloads, and tools can speed up penetration testing. It only requires filling in basic information such as the target IP address, port number, and target software version.

tools.name: Metasploit: Networking
tools.description: View and manipulate network information and data transmission on a target network.

title: OWASP Top 10 (2021)
description: The OWASP Top Ten is a standard awareness document for developers and web application security, representing a broad consensus about the most critical security risks.
concept: This module maps essential security cheat sheets to each of the OWASP Top Ten 2021 categories, helping you build, design, and maintain secure applications.

tools.name: A01:2021 – Broken Access Control
tools.description: Failures in enforcing permissions allowing attackers to access unauthorized functionality or data.

commands.command: Insecure Direct Object Reference Prevention Cheat Sheet
Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet

tools.name: A02:2021 – Cryptographic Failures
tools.description: Failures related to cryptography (or lack thereof) which often lead to sensitive data exposure.

commands.command: HTTP Strict Transport Security Cheat Sheet

tools.name: A03:2021 – Injection
tools.description: Vulnerabilities where user-supplied data is not validated, filtered, or sanitized by the application.

commands.command: OS Command Injection Defense Cheat Sheet
Injection Prevention in Java Cheat Sheet
Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet

tools.name: A04:2021 – Insecure Design
tools.description: Risks related to design flaws, requiring the use of threat modeling and secure design patterns.

tools.name: A05:2021 – Security Misconfiguration
tools.description: Insecure default settings, incomplete configurations, or misconfigured HTTP headers.

commands.command: Infrastructure as Code Security Cheat Sheet
XML External Entity Prevention Cheat Sheet

tools.name: A06:2021 – Vulnerable and Outdated Components
tools.description: Exploitation of vulnerabilities in outdated third-party frameworks, libraries, and components.

commands.command: Third Party JavaScript Management Cheat Sheet

tools.name: A07:2021 – Identification and Authentication Failures
tools.description: Issues when user identities, authentication, and session management are not implemented securely.

commands.command: Choosing and Using Security Questions Cheat Sheet
JSON Web Token for Java Cheat Sheet

tools.name: A08:2021 – Software and Data Integrity Failures
tools.description: Code and infrastructure that does not protect against integrity violations.

tools.name: A09:2021 – Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
tools.description: Failures in logging and monitoring which allow attackers to further extract data or persist in systems.

tools.name: A10:2021 – Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
tools.description: Flaws where a web application fetches a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL.

commands.command: Server Side Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet

tools.name: A11:2021 – Next Steps
tools.description: Additional guidelines and next steps for maintaining secure applications.
